title: BharatSearch: The Intent Engine
subtitle: Redefining digital discovery for the diverse linguistic and cognitive landscape of India
problem_statement: Despite high internet penetration, the 'Next Billion Users' in India face a massive 'Discovery Gap.' Current search engines are optimized for English-literate, keyboard-proficient users, leaving millions struggling with complex textual interfaces, irrelevant global results, and the 'scroll-fatigue' of sifting through SEO-optimized links rather than getting direct, culturally-contextual answers.

pillars.title: The Trust-Verify Loop
pillars.insight: Indian users frequently cross-verify digital search results with WhatsApp communities due to a deep-seated skepticism of sponsored digital content.

pillars.title: Linguistic Code-Switching
pillars.insight: Users rarely search in pure Hindi or pure English; they operate in a fluid 'Hinglish' spectrum, leading to friction when engines demand formal syntax.

pillars.title: Assisted Discovery Preference
pillars.insight: There is a high cognitive tax for 'drilling down' into websites; users prefer 'Zero-Click' summaries that provide immediate utility.

artifacts.label: Strategy
artifacts.title: Defining the Challenges
questions: How might we make information accessible to users who prefer voice and visual cues over text?
How might we bridge the gap between Hinglish/vernacular queries and high-quality local data?
How might we transform 'searching' into 'solving' for essential daily utilities?

personas.name: Rajesh Kumar
personas.quote: I want the answer, not a list of websites I have to read through.
personas.bio: Rajesh runs a local hardware store. He uses his smartphone for everything but finds it hard to find specific government scheme details or GST updates because the results are too wordy and in complex English.
frustrations: Too many ads before real results

points.action: Replace the traditional 'List of Links' with 'Actionable Cards' that provide answers directly on the main page.
points.benefit: Users get what they need without clicking through five different slow websites.

points.action: Merge search results with local community ratings and 'Verified by BharatSearch' badges.
points.benefit: Increases user trust and reduces the need for external verification on social media.

points.action: Change the interface to a 'Feed-style' discovery for users who aren't sure what to search for (e.g., local trends).
points.benefit: Helps low-literacy users discover information through visual cues and icons.

points.action: Shrink the weight of the page by serving 'Lite' versions of results for users on low-bandwidth networks.
points.benefit: The engine stays fast even in rural areas with poor connectivity.

pivot.before: A text-heavy repository of indexed global links that requires high digital literacy to navigate.
pivot.pivot_logic: Shift from 'Information Indexing' to 'Contextual Problem Solving' using multimodal inputs and regional AI.
pivot.after: A hyper-local gateway that provides immediate, voice-responsive answers and direct actions tailored for the Indian user.

stages.actions: User encounters a problem (e.g., how to fix a pipe).
stages.friction: Uncertainty on what keywords to use.

stages.actions: User speaks in a mix of Hindi and English.
stages.friction: Natural language processing fails to catch local slang.

stages.actions: System parses intent and filters local data.
stages.friction: Slow page load on restricted data plans.

stages.actions: User sees a direct answer card with a 'Play Video' button.
stages.friction: Distinguishing between an ad and a real solution.
Broken links or outdated contact numbers.

content.prompts: Check Mandi prices? Apply for Aadhar? Local News?

annotations.title: Voice-First Trigger
annotations.rationale: Enlarged microphone icon placed at the natural thumb-reach zone to encourage non-textual input.

annotations.title: Contextual Language Chips
annotations.rationale: Dynamic chips that allow users to switch result languages instantly without going into settings.

annotations.title: Direct Action Buttons
annotations.rationale: Integrating 'Call', 'Pay', or 'Book' directly into the search snippet to minimize drop-offs.
